holdings.infoBox: SCHX is an equity ETF with a total of 752 individual holdings. The top holdings are NVIDIA stock at 7.42%, Apple at 6.47%, Microsoft at 4.31%, Amazon.com at 3.54%, and Alphabet at 3.22%.

overview.description: The Schwab U.S. Large-Cap ETF (SCHX) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in large cap equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index of the 750 largest US companies. SCHX was launched on Nov 3, 2009 and is issued by Charles Schwab.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: The S&P 500 will fail to break out for a third time in 18 months
data.text: Despite the S&P 500 closing at an all-time high on Friday, the index's internals and fundamentals are too weak to maintain the rally, Morgan Stanley said Monday.

data.text: The second-quarter earnings season has continued to get worse as more companies report results, increasing the likelihood of the first earnings recession in three years.

data.text: The S&P 500's performance this year has a striking resemblance to the performance of the S&P 500 in 1998, and if history is any guide, the rest of the year could be volatile.

data.title: S&P 500 closes flat as earnings season starts
data.text: The benchmark S&P 500 index ended little changed in a choppy session on Monday after Citigroup Inc (C.N) kicked off the second-quarter earnings season with a mixed report.

data.title: Tech stocks led the S&P 500 from 2,000 to 3,000. The sector may still have far to run
data.text: Stellar performance from technology stocks led the S&P 500's run from 2,000 to 3,000, but the sector's phenomenal growth may still have some gas in the tank.

data.title: S&P 500 near flat as healthcare in spotlight
data.text: The S&P 500 was little changed in afternoon trading on Thursday, with healthcare stocks mixed after the Trump administration withdrew a rule that would kill rebates.
